item_description_text:
A horse is a big animal with four strong legs, hooves, and long hair on its head, neck, and tail. Horses are mammals, like humans, who drink milk from their moms when they’re babies. A baby horse is called a foal and can stand and walk within the first hours it is born. Horses are strong runners with good balance who can bend down to sip water, eat grass(es), and even sleep while standing up. 
Today, people across the world love to ride horses and have them as pets.

[Fossil scientists think the first members of the horse family (called Equidae) lived 55 million years ago—after dinosaurs & before humans.] The types of horses we know of today have been around for more than 4 million years and first lived in North America. Around 6,000 years ago people near Kazakhstan worked with horses--using them to carry heavy loads, help farmers, and travel. 
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Domestication_of_the_horse
